title: SIG on ITA and ECAL Assessment following 2020-2021 National Budget
text:
STANDARD INTERPRETATION GUIDELINE 2020-45
INCOME TAX ACT 2015 – 2020 ENVIRONMENT AND CLIMATE ADAPTATION LEVY
(ECAL) ASSESSMENT
This Standard Interpretation Guideline (SIG) sets out Fiji Revenue and Customs Service’s (FRCS) policy and
operational practice in relation to the 2020 ECAL Assessment.
The SIG is issued with the authority of the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of FRCS.
All legislative references in this SIG are to the Income Tax Act 2015 (“ITA”) (unless otherwise stated).
This SIG is in effect from 1 August 2020 and may need to be reviewed in the event of any relevant legislative
amendments.
CONTENT
Purpose 2
Introduction 2
Legislative Analysis 3
Who is taxable? 3
What is taxable? 3
Imposition of ECAL 3
Tax Period 3
Effective Tax Rates 3
ECAL Assessment on prescribed income for tax year 2020 6
Chargeable Income in Period 1 only 6
Example 1 6
Chargeable Income in Period 2 only 6
Example 2 6
Chargeable Income in Period 1 and Period 2 7
Example 3 7
Example 4 8
Example 5 9
Appendix 10
1 | P age
A world class revenue service delivering excellence in revenue collection, border protection, trade and travel facilitation

PURPOSE
1. The purpose of this SIG is to issue a practical guidance on the 2020 ECAL assessment on employment
income for individuals.
2. This SIG is issued due to the change in ECAL rates with effect from 1 August 2020 which was announced in
the 2020-2021 National Budget, the CEO therefore considers it necessary to provide awareness and
guidelines to the taxpayer community on the legislative changes relating thereto.
INTRODUCTION
3. In the 2020-2021 National Budget, the Minister for Economy announced a reduction in the ECAL rate from
10% to 5%.
4. Since an individual’s tax year is the period 1 January – 31 December (also known as calendar year) and the
change in tax rates is effective from 1 August 2020, the special rules provided in the Income Tax (Rates of
Tax & Levies) Regulation 2016 will be used to determine the ECAL for individuals on employment income
for tax year 2020.
This is because two effective tax rates will be applied in tax year 2020:
 one being in the period 1 January – 31 July 2020 referred to as Period 1 Tax Rates; and
 the other in the period 1 August – 31 December 2020 referred to as Period 2 Tax Rates.
5. The individual’s chargeable income and the period in which it is derived will determine tax brackets and
tax rate(s) that will apply.
6. The rules for 2020 ECAL assessment including the formula is prescribed in the Income Tax (Rates of Tax
and Levies) (Amendments) Regulations 2020 (Legal Notice No. 62 of 2020) is discussed in the table below:
Rule Employment/Income Determination of ECAL
period
1 Period 1  Apply Period 1 tax rates
2 Period 2  Apply Period 2 tax rates
3 Period 1 and Period 2  Formula will be used to determine ECAL
 Income Tax = 7x + 5y
12 12
7. Examples illustrated in this SIG demonstrate the CEO’s interpretation and application on the 2020 ECAL
Assessment. The examples do not cover the infinite number of factual scenarios that may arise. The
relevant legislative provisions must be considered and applied to each case on its particular facts. That is,
conclusions should not be drawn by determining whether the facts of a particular case may be analogous
with particular examples, but rather on the basis of applying the correct tests established by the law.
8. The full text of the legislative provisions is contained in the Appendix.
2 | P age
A world class revenue service delivering excellence in revenue collection, border protection, trade and travel facilitation

LEGISLATIVE ANALYSIS
Who is taxable?
9. For the purpose of 2020 ECAL assessment, person means an individual. An individual can be either a
resident or a non-resident who can be an employee, sole trader, partner or a trustee.
10. However, if a trustee’s income is already taxed at trust Level, this income will not be double taxed in the
hands of the trustee.
What is taxable?
11. An individual’s chargeable income will be taxable. Chargeable income is defined in section 13 of ITA 2015.
It is the gross income less the total amount of allowable deductions of a person for a tax year.
12. Gross income includes three types of income, namely; employment income, business income and property
income. It does not include exempt income or other income that has been subject to withholding tax under
section 9, 10 or 11 of ITA 2015.
13. Employment income includes salary, wages, leave pay, overtime pay, bonus, commission, directors fees,
gratuity, work condition supplements or other remuneration derived by an employee in respect of
employment which is covered in section 15 of ITA 2015.
14. Business income is the net profit earned from any business activities or any other income stated in section
17 of ITA 2015.
15. Property income includes dividend, interest, royalty, rent or any other amount arising from use of property
which is covered in section 18 of ITA 2015. Dividend income will not be taxable as it is exempt income
under Income Tax (Exempt Income) Regulations 2016 (Part 5 – Exempt Dividends). Interest income will
not be included in computing chargeable income as it is subject to final withholding tax.
Imposition of ECAL
16. Section 10 of the ECAL Act 2015 provides the imposition of ECAL on chargeable income which forms part
of Social Responsibility Tax (SRT) under the ITA 2015.
17. Section 8(2) of the ITA provides for the imposition of SRT (inclusive of ECAL) on a person who has
chargeable income for a tax year at the rate or rates prescribed in the Income Tax (Rates of Tax & Levies)
Regulation 2016.
Tax Period
18. There are two tax periods defined in tax year 2020 for ECAL assessment purposes as follows:
Period 1 means the period from 1 January 2020 to 31 July 2020; and
Period 2 means the period from 1 August 2020 to 31 December 20201.
Effective Tax Rates
19. The tax rates for Period 1 is prescribed in the Income Tax (Rates of Tax and Levies) Regulations 2016. The
rates are as follows:
1 Regulation 6(2) – Income Tax (Rates of Tax and Levies) Regulation 2016.

ECAL Assessment on prescribed income for tax year 2020
21. The 2020 ECAL assessment rules for individuals is prescribed in the Income Tax (Rates of Tax and Levies)
(Amendments) Regulations 2020 (Legal Notice No. 62 of 2020).
Chargeable income in Period 1 only
22. Regulation 6(3) deals with a person earning chargeable income in period 1 and not period 2. The ECAL will
be calculated by applying period 1 tax rate which is illustrated in example 1 below.
Example 1 – Employment Income
Mr. A is employed as a Chief Financial Controller in ZCo Ltd. His salary per annum is $604,000. He resigned
in May and his last working day is 30 June 2020. He remained unemployed for the remaining of the tax
year. Mr. A’s employment income for the period 01/01/20 – 30/06/2020 is $302,000.
What will be ECAL payable on Mr. A’s income for the year 2020?
CEO’s position: Since the income is earned in period 1 only, apply period 1 tax rates. Also there will be no
apportionment of tax. The calculation of ECAL is as follows:
Step 1 Determine the chargeable income
Chargeable Income = $302,000
Step 2 Calculate ECAL
ECAL = 10% * ($302,000 - $270,000)
= 10% * $32,000
= $3,200
Therefore, the ECAL for Mr. A is $3,200 for year 2020.
Chargeable income in Period 2 only
23. Regulation 6(4) deals with a person earning chargeable income in period 2 and not period 1. The ECAL will
be calculated by applying period 2 tax rate which is illustrated in example 2 below.
Example 2 – Employment Income
Mr. D is employed as Director Human Resource in ZCo Ltd. His salary per annum is $744,000. He resumed
work on 01/08/2020. His employment income for the period 01/08/17 – 31/12/17 is $310,000.
What will be ECAL payable on Mr. D’s income for the year 2020?
CEO’s position: Since the income is earned in period 2 only, apply period 2 tax rates. Also there will be no
apportionment of tax. The calculation of ECAL is as follows:
Step 1 Determine the chargeable income
Chargeable Income = $310,000
6 | P age
A world class revenue service delivering excellence in revenue collection, border protection, trade and travel facilitation

Step 2 Calculate ECAL
ECAL = $1,500 + [5% * ($310,000 - $300,000)]
= $1,500 + [5% * $10,000]
= $1,500 + $500
= $2,000
Therefore, the ECAL for Mr. D is $2,000 for year 2020
Chargeable income in Period 1 and Period 2
24. Regulation 6(5) deals with a person earning chargeable income in both period 1 and period 2. The formula
provided in regulation 6(5) will be used to calculate the ECAL for tax year 2020 which is as follows:
Environment Climate and Adaptation Levy = 7x + 5y
12 12
where—
“x” means the Environment Climate and Adaptation Levy computed by applying the Period 1 tax
rate to the chargeable income of the person for the tax year commencing on 1 January 2020;
“y” means the Environment Climate and Adaptation Levy computed by applying the Period 2
tax rate to the chargeable income of the person for the tax year commencing on 1 January 2020;
and
“year” means the calendar year.
25. Example 3 – Full year employment Income (Regular Income)
Mr. X is employed as Head of internal audit department in BCo Ltd. His employment income for year 2020
was $405,000.
What will be ECAL payable on Mr. X’s income for the year 2020?
CEO’s position: Since Mr.X earned income in both periods (period 1 and period 2), the formula will be used
to determine total ECAL. The calculation of ECAL is as follows:
Step 1 Determine the chargeable income
Chargeable Income = $405,000
Step 2 Calculate ECAL for each period
x = ECAL on chargeable income using Period 1 tax rates
= 10% * ($405,000 - $270,000)
= 10% * $135,000
= $13,500
y = ECAL on chargeable income using Period 2 tax rates
= $6,500 + [5% * ($405,000 - $400,000)]
= $6,500 + [5% * $5,000]
7 | P age
A world class revenue service delivering excellence in revenue collection, border protection, trade and travel facilitation

= $6,500 + $250
= $6,750
Step 3 – Use the formula to calculate ECAL
Income Tax = 7x + 5y
12 12
= 7 * $13,500 + 5 * $6,750
12 12
= $7,875 + $2,812.50
= $10,687.50
Therefore, the ECAL for Mr. X is $10,687.50 for year 2020.
Example 4 – Part Year Employment
26. Mr. H is employed as a Chief Engineer in ZCo Ltd. His salary per annum is $600,000. His employment
contract will end on 31/10/20 and will not be renewed. His employment income was as follows:
Period 1 (01/01/20 – 31/07/20) was $350,000
Period 2 (01/08/20 -31/10/20) was $150,000
What will be ECAL payable on Mr. H’s income for the year 2020?
CEO’s position: Since Mr. H earned income in both periods (period 1 and period 2), the formula will be
used to determine total ECAL. The calculation of ECAL is as follows:
Step 1 Determine the chargeable income
Chargeable Income = $350,000 + $150,000
= $500,000
Step 2 Calculate ECAL for each period
x = ECAL on chargeable income using Period 1 tax rates
= 10% * ($500,000 - $270,000)
= 10% * $230,000
= $23,000
y = ECAL on chargeable income using Period 2 tax rates
= $9,000 + [5% * ($500,000 - $450,000)]
= $9,000 + [5% * $50,000]
= $9,000 + $2,500
= $11,500
Step 3 – Use the formula to calculate ECAL
Income Tax = 7x + 5y
12 12
= 7 * $23,000 + 5 * $11,500
12 12
8 | P age
A world class revenue service delivering excellence in revenue collection, border protection, trade and travel facilitation

= $13,416.67 + $4,791.67
= $18,208.34
Therefore, the ECAL for Mr. H is $18,208.34 for year 2020.
Example 5 – Business Income
27. Mrs. J operates a sole trader business known as J Enterprise. The business operated for 2020 and the total
net profit after allowable deductions for tax purpose for the year was $708,000.
What will be ECAL payable on Mrs. J’s income for the year 2020?
CEO’s position: Since Mrs. J earned income in both periods (period 1 and period 2), the formula will be
used to determine total ECAL. The calculation of ECAL is as follows:
Step 1 Determine the chargeable income
Chargeable Income = $708,000
Step 2 Calculate ECAL for each period
x = ECAL on chargeable income using Period 1 tax rates
= 10% * ($708,000 - $270,000)
= 10% * $438,000
= $43,800
y = ECAL on chargeable income using Period 2 tax rates
= $11,500 + [5% * ($708,000 - $500,000)]
= $11,500 + [5% * $208,000]
= $11,500 + $10,400
= $21,900
Step 3 – Use the formula to calculate ECAL
Income Tax = 7x + 5y
12 12
= 7 * $43,800 + 5 * $21,900
12 12
= $25,550 + $9,125
= $34,675
Therefore, the ECAL for Mrs. J is $34,675 for year 2020.
28. For further information and clarification in regard to this SIG, please email us at tipu@frcs.org.fj
